optimization/encoding:
* **Data Encoding Method: Surface-based preprocessing**
  * The study employed DPABISurf for both structural and functional image preprocessing.
  * Structural image processing involved spatial adaptive nonlocal mean filtering for noise removal, intensity correction, skull-stripping, and brain tissue segmentation to differentiate cerebrospinal fluid (CSF), white matter (WM), and gray matter (GM).
  * Functional image processing included skull-stripping, slice timing correction, head motion correction, and nuisance correction by regressing out WM and CSF mean time series using FREESURFER-segmented masks.
  * Additional steps included band-pass temporal filtering (0.01–0.1 Hz), boundary-based registration (BBR) for aligning structural and functional images, and projection of preprocessed volume-based functional images onto the standard cortical surface (fsaverage5).
  * The ReHo measurement used Kendall’s coefficient of concordance (KCC) applied to all vertices on both hemispheres, followed by Gaussian spatial smoothing with a 10-mm full-width half maximum Gaussian kernel.
